beats.location_name: First Hill Yoga Studio (First Hill, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Jack refuels between classes with water and a banana. It's a sunny day so he's mindful of hydration.

beats.location_name: First Hill Yoga Studio (First Hill, Seattle)

beats.reasoning: Jack takes a walk down the hill to Pike Place on this gorgeous sunny day; he prefers walking when weather permits and carries his film camera to capture the light.

beats.activity: Lunch at Pike Place, photograph market vendors
beats.reasoning: Jack grabs fresh lunch from the market and shoots a few frames with his film camera. The sunny weather and bustling crowd energize him, though he avoids the crowded bars showing the FIFA match.

beats.activity: Walk back to First Hill studio
beats.location_name: First Hill Yoga Studio (First Hill, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Jack returns uphill to the studio for his afternoon teaching schedule. Another walk on this perfect June day.

beats.location_name: First Hill Yoga Studio (First Hill, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Back-to-back afternoon classes keep Jack in his element. By now the FIFA match has finished; he's focused on his students.

beats.activity: Drive to Green Lake for evening walk and fly fishing prep
beats.reasoning: Jack heads north to Green Lake to enjoy the sunny afternoon and scout fishing conditions for a potential evening outing. Light Rail closure doesn't affect his route.

beats.activity: Walk around Green Lake, scout fishing spots, take photos
beats.reasoning: Jack takes advantage of the pristine weather to walk the lake and photograph the golden afternoon light. He checks fishing conditions but decides to skip actual fishing tonight—content to soak in the day.
Jack heads home as evening approaches, energized by a full day of work and outdoor time.

beats.activity: Prepare dinner, paint in home studio
beats.reasoning: Jack unwinds with cooking and painting—two of his favorite pastimes. The long daylight and calm mood set a reflective tone for the evening.

diary:
Monday hit different today. Started with my usual ritual—coffee and stretches at home while the city was still quiet. The drive to First Hill felt smooth; I was thinking about the World Cup match everyone's buzzing about, but I had back-to-back classes to focus on. The 6 and 7:30 sessions were solid, good energy from the regulars. My noon wellness client brought some heavy stuff to the mat—we worked through it together.

Around lunchtime I walked down to Pike Place. The market was perfect in that early June sunshine, vendors animated, and I spent more time than planned photographing the flower stalls and fish throwers. Something about that chaos grounds me. Afternoon classes went well. Then I headed to Green Lake for the evening—scouted a few new fishing spots along the water, the light was golden and forgiving. Got home, threw together a simple dinner, and spent the rest of the night painting in my studio. Nothing finished, but that's okay. Felt present all day.
thought_process: The Light Rail closure didn't really affect my route today, but it was in the back of my mind—made me grateful for my car and the flexibility it gives me. I noticed the World Cup energy downtown, bars opening early, but it didn't pull at me; I'm not really a sports guy, more about being present with what I'm doing. The day had a natural rhythm: teaching grounded me, the market lunch broke the routine, and the evening walk at Green Lake felt like a reward for staying focused. My mood shifted from purposeful in the morning to reflective by evening. I think seeing clients work through their stuff, plus that light at Pike Place, reminded me why I do this—it's about presence and small moments of beauty, not productivity metrics.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
